title: permissions errors on working directory when multiple containers attach the same volumes.
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

tl;dr : When multiple containers attach to the same volumes, only the last container has access to the working directory when working directory within said volume.

For local troubleshooting of a production application we have a docker-compose file for which I use podman-compose to bring up the test environment. 

I created a test that can reproduce the error below.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Either use the above `docker-compose.yaml` or the above podman commands to reproduce the environment

podman-compose:

```
podman-compose up -d
```

docker-compose.yaml

```yaml
version: '3.7'

services:
  auth_dev: &app
    image: "alpine:latest"
    volumes:
      - .:/test:Z
    working_dir: /test/wrkdir
    ports:
      - 8081:8081
    command: 'sleep 1000000'
  mock-asset-api:
    <<: *app
    ports:
      - 8082
  mock-search-api:
    <<: *app
    ports:
      - 8083
  mock-event-api:
    <<: *app
    ports:
      - 8084
```

podman:

```
podman pod create --name=test --share net -p 8084 -p 8082 -p 8083 -p 8081:8081
a3fb02591d068b751fbd4d0ceda56b511d6eae711a7e0dac8c91ca39bd935eb7
0
podman run --name=test_auth_dev_1 -d --pod=test --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=test --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=auth_dev --mount type=bind,source=/home/rustysysdev/test/.,destination=/test,bind-propagation=Z --add-host auth_dev:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_auth_dev_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-asset-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-asset-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-search-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-search-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-event-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-event-api_1:127.0.0.1 -w /test/wrkdir alpine:latest sleep 1000000
d2fc99113cf3866645548907a9820a8e72edc437905af899c034806efc61cb44
0
podman run --name=test_mock-asset-api_1 -d --pod=test --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=test --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=mock-asset-api --mount type=bind,source=/home/rustysysdev/test/.,destination=/test,bind-propagation=Z --add-host auth_dev:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_auth_dev_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-asset-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-asset-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-search-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-search-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-event-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-event-api_1:127.0.0.1 -w /test/wrkdir alpine:latest sleep 1000000
374a59c3044549dbcc3146c985ffedb5843f2393d576e06530bd9c78323cfd25
0
podman run --name=test_mock-search-api_1 -d --pod=test --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=test --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=mock-search-api --mount type=bind,source=/home/rustysysdev/test/.,destination=/test,bind-propagation=Z --add-host auth_dev:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_auth_dev_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-asset-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-asset-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-search-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-search-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-event-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-event-api_1:127.0.0.1 -w /test/wrkdir alpine:latest sleep 1000000
f2421c17d60c3b9d4e6b4efc2a1784189555658398a42cc34f6012e94edd4498
0
podman run --name=test_mock-event-api_1 -d --pod=test --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=test --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=mock-event-api --mount type=bind,source=/home/rustysysdev/test/.,destination=/test,bind-propagation=Z --add-host auth_dev:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_auth_dev_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-asset-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-asset-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-search-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-search-api_1:127.0.0.1 --add-host mock-event-api:127.0.0.1 --add-host test_mock-event-api_1:127.0.0.1 -w /test/wrkdir alpine:latest sleep 1000000
c58296ab0bd5b159029ae7960453519306943a12009afa8a88a3c5454516593b
0
```

2.  try to `ls` for each container and see the last one started is able to perform `ls`

```
[rustysysdev@localhost test]$ podman exec -ti test_auth_dev_1 ls
ls: .: Permission denied
Error: exec session exited with non-zero exit code 1: OCI runtime error
[rustysysdev@localhost test]$ podman exec -ti test_mock-asset-api_1 ls
ls: .: Permission denied
Error: exec session exited with non-zero exit code 1: OCI runtime error
[rustysysdev@localhost test]$ podman exec -ti test_mock-event-api_1 ls
emptyfile.yaml
[rustysysdev@localhost test]$ podman exec -ti test_mock-search-api_1 ls
ls: .: Permission denied
Error: exec session exited with non-zero exit code 1: OCI runtime error 
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Permission denied for all but the last contianer started.

**Describe the results you expected:**

ls output for each container.

**Additional information**

**security context of directory:**

```
[rustysysdev@localhost test]$ ls -laZ
total 4
drwxr-xr-x. 1 rustysysdev hugetlbfs system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c852,c870  50 Aug 31 23:41 .
drwx------. 1 rustysysdev hugetlbfs unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_dir_t:s0        762 Aug 31 23:41 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 rustysysdev hugetlbfs system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c852,c870 357 Aug 31 23:41 docker-compose.yaml
drwxr-xr-x. 1 rustysysdev hugetlbfs system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c852,c870  28 Aug 31 23:42 wrkdir
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 09:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
[rustysysdev@localhost auth-gateway]$ rpm -q podman
```

Yes

comments.body:
From the podman create man page
> To change a label in the container context, you can add either of two suffixes :z or :Z to the volume mount. These suffixes tell Podman to relabel file objects on the shared volumes. The z option tells Podman that two containers share the volume content. As a result, Podman labels the content with a shared content label. Shared volume labels allow all containers to read/write content. The Z option tells Podman to label the content with a private unshared label. Only the current container can use a private volume.

so you need a lower case `z`

> From the podman create man page
> 
> > To change a label in the container context, you can add either of two suffixes :z or :Z to the volume mount. These suffixes tell Podman to relabel file objects on the shared volumes. The z option tells Podman that two containers share the volume content. As a result, Podman labels the content with a shared content label. Shared volume labels allow all containers to read/write content. The Z option tells Podman to label the content with a private unshared label. Only the current container can use a private volume.
> 
> so you need a lower case `z`

just saw that... sorry closing!
